2605.04888.title: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Tweet Sentiment Classification: A Case Study on the Sentiment140 Dataset
2605.04888.paper_summary: The exponential growth of social media has created an urgent need for automated systems to analyze unstructured public sentiment in real time. This study compares a traditional Logistic Regression model using TF-IDF features with a deep learning Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) architecture on a 10,000-tweet subset of the Sentiment140 dataset. Experimental results show that Logistic Regression outperformed BiLSTM, achieving an accuracy of 73.5% compared with 69.17%, while the deep learning model exhibited mild overfitting. These findings suggest that for medium-scale informal text data, classical machine learning with robust feature extraction can outperform more complex deep learning approaches. Finally, the trained models were integrated into an interactive web application using Streamlit and deployed on Hugging Face Spaces for public access.
2605.04888.paper_summary_zh: 社交媒體的指數增長創造了對於自動化系統的迫切需求，以實時分析非結構化的公共情感。<lb/>本研究將使用TF-IDF特徵的傳統邏輯回歸模型與深度學習的雙向長短期記憶（BiLSTM）架構進行比較，數據集為Sentiment140的10,000條推文子集。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，邏輯回歸的表現超過了BiLSTM，準確率達到73.5%，而BiLSTM為69.17%，同時深度學習模型表現出輕微的過擬合。<lb/>這些發現表明，對於中等規模的非正式文本數據，傳統機器學習結合穩健的特徵提取可以超越更複雜的深度學習方法。<lb/>最後，訓練好的模型被整合到一個使用Streamlit的互動網頁應用中，並部署在Hugging Face Spaces上以供公眾訪問。
2605.04888.author: Vita Anggraini et.al.
2605.04888.authors: Vita Anggraini, Cintya Bella, Bastian, Luluk Muthoharoh, Ardika Satria, Martin C. T. Manullang
